title: Eliminate defer/recover from send() hot path — PLAN CAREFULLY
description: MEDIUM RISK — requires careful planning. This is the single biggest performance win available. Replace panic/recover NLR with explicit unwinding/unwindTarget/unwindValue fields on Interpreter. Check unwinding flag after each runFrame() return, pop frames until target reached. Remove all 3 defer/recover blocks from Execute(), send(), sendSuper(). Touches the most critical code path in the VM — must not break any existing NLR behavior. Plan should include: detailed frame lifecycle analysis, benchmark baseline, rollback strategy. See docs/research/vm-internals-research.md TODO 5.
close_reason: Implemented: replaced panic/recover NLR with flag-based unwinding. 13.72% geomean perf improvement.

title: Assign MetaclassClass on VM — metaclass infrastructure mostly exists
description: ClassVTable with parent-chain inheritance already works (vm/class.go:369-387). The MetaclassClass field exists on VM struct (vm/vm.go:27) but is never assigned. Create metaclass objects lazily by reusing existing ClassVTable as the metaclass VTable. See docs/research/language-features-research.md TODO 14.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Wire rehydration into mag sync pull + add dependency verification
description: After mag sync pull indexes chunks, call pipeline.RehydrateFromStore to compile synced code. Add verifyCompleteness() that walks each class digest's MethodHashes and checks all exist in local store. Report gaps. Extend TestEndToEnd_PushPull to verify rehydrated class is callable. Add new integration test: VM-A compiles real .mag source, pushes to VM-B, VM-B rehydrates and executes.
close_reason: Implemented in worktree, full E2E rehydration test passing

title: Add mag sync list/diff/show visibility commands
description: Add three subcommands to cmd/mag/sync.go: (1) 'mag sync list' — show all local content grouped by type (classes with method counts, methods with owning class). Needs AllClassDigests()/AllMethods() on ContentStore. (2) 'mag sync diff <peer>' — call List RPC on peer, compute symmetric difference, display what each side has. (3) 'mag sync show <hash-prefix>' — git-style short prefix lookup via ContentStore.LookupByPrefix(). Display class metadata or method source. Add LookupByPrefix, AllClassDigests, AllMethods to vm/content_store.go.
close_reason: Implemented in worktree, list/diff/show commands, 4 new tests, all passing

title: Break up ObjectRegistry into generic TypedRegistry[K,V] — PLAN CAREFULLY
description: MEDIUM RISK — requires careful planning. ObjectRegistry is 1071 lines with 25+ registries and ~50 sync primitives. Zero cross-registry deps found. Define generic TypedRegistry[K comparable, V any] with Get/Put/Delete/GC/ForEach. Migrate incrementally from least-used (CUE, JSON) to most-used (strings, dictionaries). Plan should include: complete caller audit per registry, GC coordination analysis, migration order with rollback points. See docs/research/architecture-research.md TODO 9.
close_reason: Implemented TypedRegistry[K,V] generic, migrated all 20 registries. Net -268 lines. Zero benchmark regression.

title: Add Resolve and List RPCs to sync proto
description: Add two new RPCs to proto/maggie/v1/sync.proto: (1) Resolve(ResolveRequest{class_name}) returns ResolveResponse{root_hash, found} — resolve class FQN to content hash. (2) List(ListRequest{}) returns ListResponse{method_hashes, class_hashes, class_names} — enumerate all content on a node. Implement both in server/sync_service.go. Regenerate proto with 'cd proto && buf generate'. Wire Resolve into mag sync pull so it accepts class names (not just hex hashes).
close_reason: Implemented in worktree, 5 new tests, all 23 sync tests passing

title: Default sandboxed execution for rehydrated code
description: After RehydrateFromStore installs received code into the VM, it currently runs with full trust. Make sandboxed execution the default path for received code: (1) Track which classes were installed via rehydration (not loaded from local source). (2) When instantiating rehydrated classes, provide a helper that runs them inside forkRestricted: with restrictions derived from the maggie.toml sync.capabilities list. (3) The three security layers should compose automatically: capability manifest gates acceptance, hash verification ensures integrity, forkRestricted sandboxes execution. This does NOT need to be mandatory — trusted code from known peers can opt out — but should be the easy/default path.
close_reason: Sandbox class with run:/restrictions/isRehydrated: + VM tracking

title: Write demo Maggie source for two-VM code sharing + messaging
description: Write the actual .mag files and maggie.toml configs for the demo scenario: VM-A side: (1) Defines a Greeter class (greet: name returns 'Hello, ' , name). (2) Starts sync server, pushes Greeter to VM-B. (3) Starts an HttpServer to receive messages back. VM-B side: (1) Pulls code from VM-A (or receives push). (2) Rehydrates Greeter class. (3) Runs Greeter in a sandboxed fork (forkRestricted:). (4) Sends the result back to VM-A via HttpClient. Include maggie.toml for each VM with sync config (listen addr, peer addr, capabilities). The demo should be launchable with a single docker-compose up.
close_reason: Demo source: Greeter.mag, vm-a/Main.mag, vm-b/Main.mag with sandbox + HTTP messaging

title: Reduce string registry contention — single RLock in primitiveEQ
description: LOW RISK (immediate fix). Hold one RLock across both string lookups in primitiveEQ instead of acquiring twice. Currently does two mutex-guarded map lookups per string comparison. See docs/research/vm-internals-research.md TODO 8 for analysis. Longer-term: 16-shard registry (separate issue).
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Add ParseMethodDef to compiler for single method parsing
description: Add a function to parse a single method definition from source text (e.g. 'method: hello [ ^42 ]') without requiring a full SourceFile. Options: (A) expose the parser's method-parsing entry point directly, or (B) wrap source in a dummy class and delegate to ParseSourceFileFromString. Create compiler/parse_method.go with ParseMethodDef(source string) (*MethodDef, error). Add tests.
close_reason: Implemented in worktrees, tests passing

title: Implement RehydrateFromStore in pipeline package
description: Create pipeline/rehydrate.go with RehydrateFromStore that compiles synced content from ContentStore into runnable classes. Four phases mirroring compileAll in pipeline/pipeline.go: (1) collect class digests not yet in ClassTable, (2) topological sort by superclass, (3) create class skeletons + register in Globals, (4) compile methods via ParseMethodDef + CompileMethodDefWithContext + install in VTable + verify content hash matches. Re-index compiled methods in ContentStore replacing stubs. Add pipeline/rehydrate_test.go with unit tests: populate ContentStore with stubs manually, rehydrate, verify classes exist and methods execute.
close_reason: Implemented in worktree, 6 tests passing, 5-phase rehydration pipeline

title: Write demo launch script with step-by-step output
description: Create a scripts/demo-two-vm.sh that orchestrates the demo end-to-end: (1) Builds Docker images. (2) Starts both containers via docker-compose. (3) Waits for both sync servers to be healthy (mag sync status / ping). (4) Triggers the push from VM-A to VM-B. (5) Triggers VM-B to run the received code and send a message back. (6) Prints the received message on VM-A. (7) Tears down containers. Each step should print clear status output so the demo is followable. Include a --keep flag to leave containers running for manual exploration.
close_reason: Launch script + demo README with architecture diagram and security model docs

title: Two-VM Docker Demo Epic
description: End-to-end demo: two Maggie VMs in Docker containers sharing code via content-addressed sync and exchanging messages, with sandboxed execution of received code using all three security layers (capability manifests, hash verification, process-level restriction).
close_reason: All subtasks complete — HttpClient, DiskCache, Sandbox, Docker, demo code, .mag stubs, launch script, docs

title: Add sorted/hashed collections to core library (Set, Bag, SortedCollection, OrderedDictionary)
description: LOW-MEDIUM RISK. Phase 1: Add Go-side sort: primitive to Array. Phase 2: Set in pure Maggie backed by Dictionary. Phase 3: Bag backed by Dictionary with counts. Phase 4: OrderedDictionary (may need Go backing). Phase 5: SortedCollection using sort primitive. Check existing collection protocols (do:, select:, collect:, inject:into:) and ensure new collections conform. See docs/research/language-features-research.md TODO 17.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Remove become: from improvement plan (already implemented)
description: Research found that become: is already fully implemented at vm/object.go:324-357 with both two-way Become() and one-way BecomeForward(). Primitives registered at vm/object_primitives.go:275-307. Update docs/plans/2026-03-03-improvement-plan.md TODO 16 to reflect this.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Add sync.RWMutex to VM.Globals map
description: LOW RISK. Wrap vm.Globals with sync.RWMutex on VM struct. Main interpreter takes write lock, forked interpreters take read lock. See docs/research/vm-internals-research.md TODO 4 for analysis. Straightforward mechanical change.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Add disk cache for ContentStore persistence
description: Create vm/dist/cache.go with DiskCache struct. Git-style layout: .maggie/cache/<2-char-prefix>/<full-hex-hash>.chunk. Each file is CBOR-encoded Chunk (reuse MarshalChunk/UnmarshalChunk). API: Has/Get/Put/AllHashes/LoadInto(store)/SaveFrom(store). Integration: load cache on startup in cmd/mag/main.go, save after sync pull in cmd/mag/sync.go. Content-addressed = no invalidation needed. Add vm/dist/cache_test.go.
close_reason: Implemented in worktree, 11 tests passing

title: Add .mag stub files for HttpServer, HttpClient, HttpRequest, HttpResponse
description: The HTTP primitives are registered in Go (vm/http_primitives.go) but have no .mag wrapper files in lib/. Add stub .mag files with class declarations and primitive method stubs so the API is discoverable from Maggie code and works with fileOut/documentation tools. Follow the pattern used by UnixSocketServer.mag and SocketConnection.mag in lib/.
close_reason: HttpClient/HttpServer/HttpRequest/HttpResponse .mag stubs created

title: Enrich class chunk Content with full structural metadata
description: ClassToChunk currently stores only the class name as Content. Change it to serialize full ClassDigest metadata (namespace, superclass, ivars, cvars, docstring) using a line-oriented text format (CLASS/NAMESPACE/SUPER/IVARS/CVARS/DOC tags). Add EncodeClassContent/DecodeClassContent functions to vm/dist/chunker.go. Update all callsites: cmd/mag/sync.go, server/sync_service.go, server/sync_integration_test.go. Update chunker_test.go for new format.
close_reason: Implemented in worktrees, tests passing

title: Add peephole optimizer pass — PLAN CAREFULLY
description: MEDIUM RISK — requires careful planning. Constant folding is UNSOUND in Smalltalk (can't guarantee receiver type). Focus on: PUSH_SELF+RETURN_TOP -> RETURN_SELF, dead PUSH+POP elimination, unreachable code after OpJump. Main complexity is jump offset fixup when bytes are removed. Plan should include: full opcode catalog analysis, list of safe peephole patterns, jump fixup algorithm design, benchmark targets. See docs/research/architecture-research.md TODO 12.

title: Fix broken exception pass implementation (double-pops handler)
description: Research found that the current pass implementation in exception handling is broken — it double-pops the handler stack. The retry is also a stub. Fix pass to correctly pop current handler and re-signal. See docs/research/language-features-research.md TODO 15 for detailed analysis of the handler stack linked list and recommended fix using new panic types.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Add HttpClient primitive for outbound HTTP
description: Maggie has HttpServer/HttpRequest/HttpResponse but no outbound HTTP client. Add an HttpClient class with at least: get: url, post:body:, post:body:contentType:. Wrap Go's net/http.Client. Register as VM primitive in http_primitives.go. This is required for VM-to-VM messaging in the demo — one VM's Maggie code needs to call another VM's HTTP endpoint.
close_reason: HttpClient primitive implemented with get:/post:body:/put:body:/delete: methods

title: Eliminate popN() slice allocation with pre-allocated arg buffer
description: LOW RISK. Add [16]Value pre-allocated argument buffer to Interpreter struct (popNFast). 3 of 5 callers (send, sendSuper, OpTailSend) consume args immediately and are safe for zero-alloc sub-slice. Keep allocating popN for OpCreateBlock and OpCreateArray. Run BenchmarkHotPath before/after. See docs/research/vm-internals-research.md TODO 6.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Use TryFromSmallInt for BigInt overflow detection
description: TryFromSmallInt() already exists at vm/value.go:203. Marker 10 is free in vm/markers.go for BigInt values. Wrap math/big.Int in BigIntObject and add to ObjectRegistry. See docs/research/language-features-research.md TODO 13 for full design.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Create Dockerfile and docker-compose for two-VM demo
description: Create Docker infrastructure for running two Maggie VMs on separate containers: (1) Dockerfile that builds the mag binary and bundles the default image. Multi-stage build: Go builder stage + minimal runtime stage. (2) docker-compose.yml with two services (vm-a, vm-b) on a shared network, each with its own maggie.toml, source directory, and exposed sync port. vm-a listens on :8081, vm-b on :8082. Each can reach the other by service name.
close_reason: Dockerfile, docker-compose, per-VM maggie.toml configs created

title: Extend Chunk struct with method metadata fields
description: Add Selector (string), ClassName (string, owning class FQN), and IsClassSide (bool) fields to vm/dist/chunk.go Chunk struct with CBOR tags 6/7/8. Update MethodToChunk in chunker.go to populate these from CompiledMethod. Open question: decide whether to also add Namespace/Imports fields or rely on ClassDigest.Namespace (ClassDigest currently has no Imports field). If bare class names in source need re-resolution, either add Imports []string to ClassDigest or require synced source uses FQNs.
close_reason: Implemented in worktrees, tests passing

title: Flatten VTables for O(1) dispatch — PLAN CAREFULLY
description: LOW-MEDIUM RISK — requires careful planning. Lazy rebuild with dirty bit: add flat []Method field to VTable, rebuild on first lookup after AddMethod dirties it. ~720KB for ~180 classes (negligible). Must audit all places that modify VTables after class creation: AddMethod, AddPrimitiveMethod, AddClassMethod, trait inclusion. Plan should include: enumeration of all VTable mutation sites, memory impact analysis, benchmark comparison for polymorphic dispatch. See docs/research/vm-internals-research.md TODO 7.
close_reason: Implemented flat VTable with lazy rebuild for O(1) dispatch

title: Wire emitSourcePos() calls into compiler codegen
description: Source map infrastructure already exists end-to-end (SourceLoc struct, SourceMap field on CompiledMethod/BlockMethod, image serialization at 12 bytes/entry). What's missing is emission: the Compiler never calls emitSourcePos during codegen. Add emitSourcePos(node) at top of compileExpr and compileStmt, then enrich StackTrace() with method.SourceLocation(frame.IP). See docs/research/architecture-research.md TODO 11.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main

title: Extract compilation pipeline from cmd/mag/main.go — PLAN CAREFULLY
description: MEDIUM RISK — requires careful planning. main.go is 1523 lines. 13 of 24 functions are pure compilation logic with no CLI deps. Extract pipeline/ package (CompileAll, CompilePath, LoadProject, CollectFiles) and repl/ package. main.go becomes ~200-line thin dispatcher. Plan should include: complete dependency graph of main.go functions, identification of CLI-coupled state that needs parameterizing, package API signatures, test strategy for extracted packages. See docs/research/architecture-research.md TODO 10.
close_reason: Extracted pipeline/ package from cmd/mag/main.go: 13 functions moved, main.go 1524→865 lines, all tests pass

title: Wire DiskCache into CLI startup and sync pull
description: vm/dist/cache.go has a fully implemented DiskCache (git-style .maggie/cache/ layout) but it's never called from cmd/mag/main.go or cmd/mag/sync.go. Wire it in: (1) On startup, call cache.LoadInto(vmInst.ContentStore()) to restore previously synced content. (2) After sync pull, call cache.SaveFrom(store) to persist received chunks. Without this, pulled code vanishes on process restart.
close_reason: DiskCache wired into startup, sync pull, and Transfer RPC
